skill.name: tailwind-css-v4-mastery
skill.description: Guides Tailwind CSS V4 migrations, CSS-first configuration, component systems, and Oxide engine performance work.
skill.summary: Use Tailwind CSS V4 with clear migration, theming, component, and performance guidance.
skill.author: Calel33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command, network, filesystem, and environment patterns. Manual review found most are Markdown examples or public documentation links, but the bundled migration script can install packages and rewrite project files. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, obfuscation, or confirmed malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: Optional migration script modifies projects and installs packages
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The bundled shell script accepts a project directory, installs Tailwind packages, edits CSS and template files, and removes tailwind.config.js after backup. This matches the skill purpose, but it can change many files and should be run only in reviewed, version-controlled projects.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script directly performs npm installs, recursive find operations, sed rewrites, and file deletion. The behavior is legitimate migration automation, but the operational impact is clear.

low_findings.title: Markdown examples triggered command and crypto detections
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Backticked Tailwind utilities, shell examples, and terms such as shadow or md appear in documentation. They are examples and reference text, not runtime code execution or weak cryptographic use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is documentation syntax and Tailwind class naming. I found no code path that executes those Markdown snippets automatically.

low_findings.title: External URLs are public Tailwind resources
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Hardcoded URLs point to Tailwind documentation, the Tailwind GitHub project, the Tailwind playground, and the Tailwind Discord. No evidence found that the skill sends data to these URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are listed under resource sections and are not used by executable code. They match legitimate Tailwind project resources.

low_findings.title: Environment variable reference is a build example
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The process.env.NODE_ENV text appears inside a Vite configuration example for production builds. No evidence found of secret reading, environment dumping, or network transmission.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reference is static documentation inside a code block. It uses a fixed production string and does not access sensitive environment variables.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unpinned package installation in migration script
dangerous_patterns.description: The script installs tailwindcss@latest and Tailwind plugins. This is expected for migration tooling, but it relies on current package registry state at execution time.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The npm install commands are explicit and executable. The risk is supply-chain and reproducibility risk, not confirmed malicious behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Recursive in-place file rewriting
dangerous_patterns.description: The script recursively finds CSS, SCSS, HTML, JSX, TSX, and Vue files, then rewrites matching Tailwind imports and utility names in place.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The sed rewrites are visible and affect user project files. The patterns are fixed and migration-related, so risk is bounded to unintended edits.

dangerous_patterns.title: Configuration file removal after backup
dangerous_patterns.description: The migration script removes tailwind.config.js after copying selected files into a backup directory. This is expected for Tailwind V4, but it can surprise users.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The backup and removal steps are explicit. The action is documented, but it remains a potentially destructive filesystem operation.

content.user_title: Master Tailwind CSS V4 Migration and Design Systems
content.value_statement: Tailwind CSS V4 changes configuration, build tooling, and utility behavior. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured guidance for migration, theming, components, and performance.
actual_capabilities: Explains Tailwind V4 CSS-first configuration and @theme usage.
Guides V3 to V4 migration planning and utility refactoring.
Provides an optional shell script for common migration edits.
Documents breaking changes, browser requirements, and package choices.
Covers component system patterns using CSS variables and Tailwind utilities.
Gives performance guidance for the Oxide engine and build tooling.
limitations: The migration script can change many files and needs human review.
The references may become outdated as Tailwind V4 evolves.
It does not replace testing in each target framework or browser.
It focuses on Tailwind V4, not general frontend architecture.

use_cases.title: Plan a Tailwind V4 upgrade
use_cases.description: Audit an existing V3 project, identify breaking changes, and plan a controlled V4 migration.

use_cases.title: Design a CSS-first theme system
use_cases.description: Create token-driven themes with @theme, CSS variables, and reusable component conventions.

use_cases.title: Improve Tailwind build performance
use_cases.description: Choose the right Tailwind V4 integration and review Oxide engine performance practices.

prompt_templates.title: Explain Tailwind V4 basics
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain the main Tailwind CSS V4 changes for a developer who knows Tailwind V3.

prompt_templates.title: Review my migration plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Tailwind V3 to V4 migration plan and list risks, missing steps, and validation checks.

prompt_templates.title: Design a theme architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Tailwind V4 @theme strategy for a multi-brand component system with light and dark modes.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose V4 performance issues
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this Tailwind V4 build setup and recommend changes to improve build time and HMR performance.

output_examples.input: Migrate a V3 app with custom colors, old opacity utilities, and a Vite setup.
output_examples.output: A staged migration checklist, package guidance, utility rename notes, testing steps, and rollback advice.

output_examples.input: Create a Tailwind V4 design system for three product brands.
output_examples.output: A token structure, theme layering plan, component conventions, and validation checklist for brand switching.

output_examples.input: My Tailwind V4 rebuilds feel slow in development.
output_examples.output: A focused review of plugin choice, content scope, source maps, CSS organization, and benchmark steps.

best_practices: Run the migration script only after committing or backing up the project.
Translate JavaScript theme settings into clear CSS variables before refactoring components.
Validate browser support, dark mode, responsive states, and production builds after each migration stage.
anti_patterns: Do not run automated rewrites on an uncommitted project.
Do not keep large Tailwind V3 configuration files as the source of truth.
Do not assume V3 utility names and defaults behave the same in V4.

faq.question: Does this skill support Tailwind V3?
faq.answer: It focuses on Tailwind V4 and V3 to V4 migration. Use general Tailwind help for older projects.

faq.question: Can it run an automated migration?
faq.answer: It includes a shell script for common migration edits. Review changes carefully before and after running it.

faq.question: Is the migration script safe for production projects?
faq.answer: It can modify many files and install packages. Run it only in a backed-up or version-controlled workspace.

faq.question: Does it explain @theme configuration?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill covers CSS-first configuration, token mapping, custom themes, and component-scoped patterns.

faq.question: Does it include performance guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers plugin selection, Oxide engine expectations, build settings, and measurement practices.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
